notes: Most often used for fleet maintenance, repair and service applications
Lift unit translates to the rear approximately 36 1/8 during vertical articulation
Remove all sharp corners & edges unless otherwise specified
Welding medium shall conform to AWS specifications
Use smooth electrical 90's in conduits, not plumbing 90's
Seal barrier at each conduit going to lift unit (by others)
All floor requirements are based on concrete slab supported by soil
Installation involving slab not on grade must be reviewed by building architect

pages_summary.description: General notes and surface lifts specifications
pages_summary.extracted_text: GENERAL NOTES, SURFACE LIFTS ONLY, UNITS = INCH (mm), NOTE 1 Where existing concrete floor has a minimum ultimate strength of not less than Fc= 4,000 psi (27.6 MPa) and is at least 6 inches (152) thick, then the floor system may be used without alterations, NOTE 2 If the concrete floor system does not meet minimum specifications of note 1 above, then a new concrete floor shall be installed to support the lift, NOTE 3 If the strength of an existing floor system is unknown or not documented, its strength should be determined by core samples, NOTE 4 Any new concrete used for repairs or alterations to the floor system shall be at a minimum Fc=4,000 psi (27.6 MPa), with heavy aggregate, NOTE 12 In certain cases the floor slab may have adequate strength to support the lift unit but may not be thick enough to provide the minimum embedment depth for the at WEJ-IT anchor bolts
